questions.prompt: A vendor claims adding an llms.txt file to your site is critical for AI Overview visibility. What's Google's actual position?

choices.text: Yes, llms.txt is required for AI search.
Google explicitly says llms.txt files and other 'special' markup are NOT needed. Their AI features use the same Search index as everything else, built from your normal HTML pages.
Required only for sites with over 1,000 pages.

questions.explanation: Google directly debunks this in the AI Optimization Guide: 'LLMS.txt files and other 'special' markup: You don't need to create new machine readable files, AI text files, markup, or Markdown to appear in generative AI search.' If a vendor is selling llms.txt as required for Google AI visibility, they're selling something Google has publicly said doesn't matter.

questions.prompt: A consultant pitches you on 'GEO certification' — a separate optimization discipline for AI search, distinct from classic SEO. What's the most accurate response?

choices.text: Sign up immediately — GEO is the future.

Decline. Google says explicitly: 'From Google Search's perspective, optimizing for generative AI search is optimizing for the search experience, and thus still SEO.' There is no separate GEO discipline — it's classic SEO with attention to a few new properties.
Only useful for sites in highly competitive niches.

questions.explanation: Google has been direct on this: AI search optimization isn't a separate field. The same classic SEO fundamentals — quality content, technical hygiene, authority — drive AI visibility. Consultants positioning AEO/GEO as a distinct discipline are typically selling repackaged classic SEO at a markup, or worse, selling tactics Google has explicitly debunked.
